meaning: adj.无辨别力的,迟钝的,无眼力的
examples: If their praise is censure, your censure may be praise, for they are not more undiscerning, than you are prejudiced and unjust.
There are many Satanic gimmicks, which deceptively present only part of the truth, and they are always bestsellers, because satan knows that undiscerning people always gravitate toward evil.
And it is made harder by undiscerning foreign-trade and investment policies.
